title: When Do You Need an Emergency Locksmith?
excerpt: Be Secure Locksmith outlines 7 common reasons to call an emergency locksmith. Contact us today to schedule professional locksmith services in Gainesville.
author: Netta Kaiden
metaTitle: When Do You Need an Emergency Locksmith?
metaDescription: Be Secure Locksmith outlines 7 common reasons to call an emergency locksmith. Contact us today to schedule professional locksmith services in Gainesville.
body: We are all familiar with the pocket pat-down of each pocket to make sure that you have your wallet, phone, and keys before you leave the house. Whether it’s your home or business, getting locked out of your property can be inconvenient and stressful. An emergency locksmith can be there when you need them and can give you some peace of mind when you are in a tight situation. Here are some reasons why you might need a emergency locksmith from Be Secure Locksmith Gainesville.
Looking for fast and affordable locksmiths in Gainesville, FL? Contact us today to schedule locksmith services! Whether you need a lock repaired or a key copied, we are the experts you can turn to.

It is not uncommon to lose a set of keys, as they are small and pretty easily forgotten. They may have been misplaced or could have fallen out of a pocket or a purse in the search for something else. If the wrong person finds your keys, this could cause potential security issues because there is a high chance that someone could use them to access your property. A locksmith will be able to re-key or replace your locks and then make new keys.

When moving into a new place, the new homeowner might assume that they don’t have to worry about security issues, but they don’t know how many people have copies of your keys. After making a big move, you’ll want to know that your new place is secured, and you can make sure you are the only person with keys so that the previous owners can no longer access the house.
You Have Damaged Locks Or Broken Keys

After years of use, keys can wear down and break due to metal fatigue. It is an inconvenience if the key were to snap inside the lock, which makes it difficult to remove without the proper locksmith tools. A locksmith can remove the key from within your door or replace the entire lock, and they can give you a fresh, stronger key that won’t break as easily.

A damaged lock can be a sign of an attempted robbery that can be detrimental to your safety and your business, as it is easy to gain entry to a door with a damaged lock. Over time, locks can also fail to work properly, which can lock you out of your home, car, or business. A locksmith will assist you in repairing your lock if someone has broken your locks in an attempt to obtain access to your property or if they are totally wearing out due to wear and tear.
You Got Locked Out Of Your Home Or Apartment

One of the most common reasons why a homeowner would need an emergency locksmith is accidentally getting locked out of your house or apartment. You might be tempted to take matters into your own hands when stuck outside in the freezing cold or in the pouring rain. If you do find yourself in this position, it is strongly advised that you should not attempt to access the property yourself. This could result in more collateral loss that isn’t covered by insurers, as well as personal injuries.
You Got Locked Out Of Your Car

So you’ve locked yourself out of your car, the good news is that this is another most common reasons for contacting an emergency locksmith. Don’t get caught in the embarrassing position of shimmying a coat hanger through your window. A reliable locksmith team will be able to help with emergency lockouts, get you back in your car so you can get on with your day.

Code combinations and passwords are easy to forget, so it is likely that a homeowner can forget their code combination at some point in their life. Installing electronic locks reduces the chance of forgetting the code, but it is still possible to forget. This rings especially true in cases where the code on the electronic keypad lock was recently reset. Most people avoid writing down passwords and combinations for security reasons, but we recommend writing down the code for at least the first few days after it is reset.
You Think Someone Has Broken Into Your Home

If the door looks like someone tried to force it open, first call 911 then we recommend getting a locksmith to your house as soon as possible. This is because the homeowner might not know who decided to try to enter the house and why they wanted in so badly. In cases like this, it is best to stay on the side of caution and get your locks changed and rekeyed.
